title: Farm Film and Music Series – Tending Nature
description: Summertime Film on the Farm Join us for the July Farm Film & Music where we'll screen Tending Nature: Indigenous Land Stewardship . Tending Nature is a documentary series that explores how, for a thousand years, land has provided for Native peoples and how tribal groups have actively shaped and tended (cared for) the land. Through in-person interviews, the series highlights current projects that are trying to solve ongoing environmental problems by integrating traditional knowledge from tribal groups with Western science. Join us for an inspiring evening on the Farm with live music, community partners, a panel conversation, and a special outdoor screening under the stars.   Event Details 📅 Date: Thursday, July 23 🕡 6 PM – Gates Open: Enjoy live music from Nathan and Jessie , community partners, and a beautiful Farm setting 🎙️ 7:30 PM - Panel discussion 🎬 8:05 PM –Film Screening Tickets $12/person in advance, $18/person at the door $36/person for Farm Friend Proceeds help support the Farm’s mission to nourish our community and educate about environmental sustainability. Learn more about our impact here . Tending Nature—Indigenous Land Stewardship This "Tending Nature" special features multiple perspectives and voices from Indigenous communities across California who are striving to keep the practices of their heritage alive. From coming-of-age rituals, seasonal food harvests, basket weaving and jewelry making, the documentary shares how traditional practices can be protected and maintained as a way of life for future generations. Part of Our Farm Film & Music Series This event opens Coastal Roots Farm’s 2026 Farm Film & Music Series, a seasonal community experience featuring documentary films, live local music, and meaningful conversation on the Farm. Each event invites guests to explore pressing environmental and social issues while gathering in community, enjoying live music, and connecting with local partners. By beginning with Groundswell , this year’s series starts with a message at the heart of the Farm: healthy soil, resilient communities, and collective action can change what is possible. View the full 2026 series lineup here . Thank You to our Event Sponsors Series Title Sponsor: Presenting Sponsor: Interested in supporting this powerful community series? Learn more about sponsorship opportunities here or email Sara@coastalrootsfarm.org .

title: Earth Based Judaism: Talmud, Land, and Belonging (3-Part Series)
description: In this three-session cohort , we will gather at the Farm to explore Earth-Based Judaism through Talmudic stories about creativity, home, drought, rain, and the relationship between human beings and the natural world. Together, we will read ancient texts through literary, cultural, and contemporary lenses, paying attention to the landscapes that shape them: caves, streams, trees, homes, dry earth, and falling rain. Our learning will invite reflection on belonging, memory, community, and our responsibilities to one another and the land. All adults are welcome, of any or no faith background, and of all identities, abilities, and experience levels. No need to identify as Jewish to gain and contribute meaningfully. Event Details 📅 Dates: Sundays July 26, August 2, and August 23 🕡 10a.m.-12p.m. 📍 Meet at the Coffee Cart & Walk to the Makom Esther 🎟️ $90 for the series Class Details: CLASS ONE: July 26 - Creativity in a Cave We’ll explore the famous story of Rabbi Shimon bar Yochai and ask whether creativity requires withdrawing from ordinary life, or whether it can emerge from within life itself. We’ll also pay attention to the natural landscape of the story, including the cave, the stream, and the carob tree, and consider how these elements shape the Talmud’s understanding of the relationship between human beings and the natural world. CLASS TWO: August 2 - Home Can we ever truly return home after a long absence? We’ll read both the Greek story of the Odyssey and Talmudic texts about home and belonging, and think together about home not only as a physical place but also as a relationship to land, memory, and the environment. We’ll ask how distance from the land reflects a deeper disruption in the relationship between people and their surroundings. CLASS THREE: August 23 - Drought and Rain We’ll read Talmudic stories about ancient communal practices during times of drought, explore the fascinating figures of Jewish rainmakers, and think about the connection the Talmud draws between rainfall, communal ethics, and the moral condition of society. Guest Teacher Chaya Gilboa is joining us this summer as a teaching resident. She is a PhD candidate in the Department of Literature, a Talmud scholar whose research focuses on questions of beauty and time, and has been teaching Talmud for over a decade. Alongside her academic work, she has spent many years leading social initiatives in Israel, particularly in the areas of Jewish-Arab relations and dialogue between secular and ultra-Orthodox communities. She is also a regular voice in Israeli radio and television, where she brings Jewish texts into conversation with contemporary issues. Registration is for the full series only and closes after the second session. A minimum of 10 participants is needed to run the series. We never want cost to be a barrier to participation. Scholarships are available upon request. The registration form will also include an opportunity to make an additional contribution, which will help ensure that participants relying on scholarship support are able to attend. Please email Education Manager, Sharone Oren for scholarship information.

title: Monthly Farm Tour – August 2026
description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm. On this guided tour, you’ll enjoy a gentle walk through our ever-evolving gardens, educational spaces, chicken coops, compost operations, and vibrant Food Forest. Learn about our unique growing methods and how we cultivate food, community, and justice. The tour includes a hill and mixed terrain. We recommend wearing flat shoes, a sun hat, and bringing a water bottle. Learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm. Pre-registration required. 🗓️ Sunday, August 9 🕒 10:30-12 p.m. | 📍Meet at the Coastal Roots Farm Coffee Cart 🎟️ $25/person. About Coastal Roots Farm We're nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity. Each year, we also welcome over 15,000 youth and adults for outdoor education, Jewish life programs, and community events that inspire environmental stewardship, climate action, and connection to Jewish tradition.
excerpt: Get a behind-the-scenes glimpse of Coastal Roots Farm’s production methods as you enjoy a gentle walk through the ever-evolving and diverse vegetable, herb, and flower gardens.

description: Join us at Eagle Mesa for a presentation about our bald & golden eagles! Learn about these two non-releasable raptors, like what they eat, why they reside at the Living Coast, fun facts, and more! *All events subject to cancellation at the discretion of our staff.

description: Visit the Living Coast Discovery Center to learn about the diverse range of species that inhabit it, including moon jellies, chuckwallas, snakes, owls, and more! You never know what you'll catch a glimpse of during our Wildlife Surprise. *All presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of the staff.

title: Shark & Ray Presentation
description: Visit the Shark & Ray Experience to learn about the diverse range of species that inhabit it, including leopard sharks, bat rays, diamond rays, shovelnose guitarfish, horn sharks, and more! This exhibit features a touch tank where you can interact with some of these residents while learning about them. *All presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of the staff.

description: Join us at Turtle Lagoon to learn about green sea turtles, even the ones you can find right here in San Diego Bay! A Living Coast educator will tell you all about sea turtles, like their diet, shell, fun facts, and more! *All animal presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of our staff.

title: Sweetwater Safari Hike
description: Join us every Saturday as we explore the Sweetwater Marsh Trail on the San Diego Bay National Wildlife Refuge! We will learn about the history of the Sweetwater Marsh, the salt marsh ecosystem, and the plants and wildlife thriving in this protected space. Backpacks with fun activities, including magnifying glasses and binoculars, will be available for families to use while on the hike. Check-in time: 11:30 am Duration: 30-45 Minutes Meeting Location: We will meet outside the front doors of the Living Coast Discovery Center located at 1000 Gunpowder Point Drive, Chula Vista, CA 91910. Driving directions: From the I5 freeway, exit at E street and head west to the Living Coast Discovery Center parking lot. This hike location is located on a wildlife refuge, and all visitors must park in the outer lot and take the free Living Coast Discovery Center shuttle. Suggested parking: This locations parking lot is located outside of a wildlife refuge. All visitors must take the free shuttle from the parking lot located on Gunpowder Point Drive. This lot is located at Gunpowder Point Drive, Chula Vista CA 91910. Restrooms and Water: The nearest restrooms are located inside the front doors of the Living Coast Discovery Center. You do not have to pay to use the restrooms, but if visitors choose to enter the facility to visit exhibits they must pay the admission fees. Hike lengths: This trail is approximately 1 mile in length. Program Supplies: Please make sure to wear comfortable clothes and shoes for hiking. Bringing a water bottle, bug protection, and sun protection are highly recommended. Hiking level: Easy No pets will be allowed on the hike.

title: National Zookeeper Week
description: National Zookeeper Week Saturday, July 25 & Sunday, July 26 This Place Looks Like a Zoo… Because It Is! Your Living Coast — where land meets marsh, and a whole world of local wildlife is waiting to meet you! Our Animal Care Team may be small, but their impact is huge! Working 365 days a year to keep over 80 local species of reptiles, avians, and marine life healthy and thriving. From daily feedings and health checks to cleaning habitats and maintaining exhibits, their passion and commitment shine through in everything they do. Discover what it takes to care for local wildlife and how you can make a difference.

title: Sweetwater Safari Hike
description: Join us every Saturday as we explore the Sweetwater Marsh Trail on the San Diego Bay National Wildlife Refuge! We will learn about the history of the Sweetwater Marsh, the salt marsh ecosystem, and the plants and wildlife thriving in this protected space. Backpacks with fun activities, including magnifying glasses and binoculars, will be available for families to use while on the hike. Check-in time: 11:30 am Duration: 30-45 Minutes Meeting Location: We will meet outside the front doors of the Living Coast Discovery Center located at 1000 Gunpowder Point Drive, Chula Vista, CA 91910. Driving directions: From the I5 freeway, exit at E street and head west to the Living Coast Discovery Center parking lot. This hike location is located on a wildlife refuge, and all visitors must park in the outer lot and take the free Living Coast Discovery Center shuttle. Suggested parking: This locations parking lot is located outside of a wildlife refuge. All visitors must take the free shuttle from the parking lot located on Gunpowder Point Drive. This lot is located at Gunpowder Point Drive, Chula Vista CA 91910. Restrooms and Water: The nearest restrooms are located inside the front doors of the Living Coast Discovery Center. You do not have to pay to use the restrooms, but if visitors choose to enter the facility to visit exhibits they must pay the admission fees. Hike lengths: This trail is approximately 1 mile in length. Program Supplies: Please make sure to wear comfortable clothes and shoes for hiking. Bringing a water bottle, bug protection, and sun protection are highly recommended. Hiking level: Easy No pets will be allowed on the hike.

title: Sweetwater Safari Hike
description: Join us every Saturday as we explore the Sweetwater Marsh Trail on the San Diego Bay National Wildlife Refuge! We will learn about the history of the Sweetwater Marsh, the salt marsh ecosystem, and the plants and wildlife thriving in this protected space. Backpacks with fun activities, including magnifying glasses and binoculars, will be available for families to use while on the hike. Check-in time: 11:30 am Duration: 30-45 Minutes Meeting Location: We will meet outside the front doors of the Living Coast Discovery Center located at 1000 Gunpowder Point Drive, Chula Vista, CA 91910. Driving directions: From the I5 freeway, exit at E street and head west to the Living Coast Discovery Center parking lot. This hike location is located on a wildlife refuge, and all visitors must park in the outer lot and take the free Living Coast Discovery Center shuttle. Suggested parking: This locations parking lot is located outside of a wildlife refuge. All visitors must take the free shuttle from the parking lot located on Gunpowder Point Drive. This lot is located at Gunpowder Point Drive, Chula Vista CA 91910. Restrooms and Water: The nearest restrooms are located inside the front doors of the Living Coast Discovery Center. You do not have to pay to use the restrooms, but if visitors choose to enter the facility to visit exhibits they must pay the admission fees. Hike lengths: This trail is approximately 1 mile in length. Program Supplies: Please make sure to wear comfortable clothes and shoes for hiking. Bringing a water bottle, bug protection, and sun protection are highly recommended. Hiking level: Easy No pets will be allowed on the hike.

title: CLOSED TO THE PUBLIC
description: The Living Coast will be closed to the public. We apologize for the inconvenience.

title: Farm to Bay
description: Get your tastebuds and fall cocktail attire ready for Living Coast’s premier fundraising event of the year! We’ll feature fun food, bubbly beverages, animal ambassadors, and spectacular sunset views, all in support of our mission to inspire the community to connect with and care for our coastal environment. As part of our commitment to a thriving natural coast, a key pillar of Farm to Bay is our Zero Waste goal. Partners use compostable or reusable serving materials, and a team led by graduates from our Master Composter Training Course sorts through any waste produced throughout the event. More details about our food and beverage partners are to come , but until then Save the Date for Saturday, October 24, from 3:00-6:30 PM.

organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
title: Compost & Mulch Giveaway – 7/25 at Sun Runner Lot
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost! This compost is available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours. To increase opportunities for residents to pick up compost, I Love A Clean San Diego and the City of San Diego are partnering to host Compost Giveaways on select weekends. Get your hands on some nutrient-rich compost for your garden at no cost. Whether you're a seasoned gardener or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone looking to give their plants a boost. Swing by and grab some compost to help your garden thrive! Mulch is also available at this event. Things to bring: Shovel Gloves Containers Tarp and bungees for truck beds (ALL TRUCK BEDS MUST BE COVERED!) Things to know: Be prepared to self load compost and mulch All participants are REQUIRED to sign a release waiver Must approach from the EAST Exact Coordinates: (32.76726972788742, -117.20799017856716) Please be aware, in order to collect compost you are REQUIRED to sign a waiver. All participants will need to sign a waiver upon entering the event. About the Miramar Greenery: At the Miramar Greenery, compost is made from yard trimmings and food scraps that have been ground, placed in windrows, turned and watered for 70 days. During this time, microorganisms digest the carbon and nitrogen rich mixture, causing the windrow to sustain temperatures of 140-165 F. This process eliminates most weed seeds and pathogens while breaking down the organic material into beneficial soil nutrients. Finished compost is screened to a particle size of one-half inch or less which also removes film plastic from the final product. For more information, visit https://www.sandiego.gov/environmental-services/miramar Practical Application and Benefits : Compost can be used as an amendment to improve soil texture and increase nutrient and water holding capacity. In San Diego, where soil lacks many essential plant nutrients, mixing compost in with the soil is one of the best things you can do for your yard and garden. Note: This event will occur rain or shine, however if severe weather occurs the days leading up to the event, the event will be rescheduled or canceled. If needed, registrants will receive electronic communication as soon as possible before the event. This event is made possible by the City of San Diego Environmental Services Department and facilitated by I Love A Clean San Diego.

The County of San Diego's Rancho Los Penasquitos - In 1823, Francisco Maria Ruiz built a modest adobe that established Rancho Los Penasquitos as San Diego County's first Mexican land grant, remnants of which still stand within the present-day ranch house. Today, the surrounding park offers more than 10 miles of multi-use trails, a staging area, restrooms, and hosts weddings, reunions, and community events. The site also features the Los Peñasquitos Community Garden, founded in 2012, which provides 20 plots for local residents to grow their own produce through seasonal leases. The historic adobe serves as a cultural landmark and museum, offering visitors guided tours, educational programs, and a glimpse into the region’s early ranching and adobe life. organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
title: From Scraps to Soil: Vermicomposting Workshop
description: Build a worm composting bin for your home and learn the importance of diverting food waste from our landfills! Learn to reduce your food waste and compost at home! Join I Love A Clean San Diego for our free From Scraps to Soil: Vermicomposting Workshop at the Alta Vista Botanical Gardens on Saturday, August 22 at 10:30 a.m. During this workshop, we’ll cover: How to reduce food waste in the kitchen Why it’s so important to keep food waste out of landfills through composting and the organic waste recycling program Tips and tricks on how to use the curbside green bin Attendees can make and take home a vermicomposting bin (worm composting) during the workshop – all supplies and information will be provided to start easily composting at home! *Please note that supplies are limited to one bin per household, with priority given to residents of the City of Vista. We’ll also have the Sprouting Station open for attendees to plant fruit and vegetable seeds to take home and grow – use the finished compost from your vermicomposting bin to fertilize! Registration is required. Thank you to the City of Vista for sponsoring this event!

organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
title: 42nd Annual Coastal Cleanup Day
description: The Ocean Conservancy and the California Coastal Commission partner with volunteer organizations, such as I Love A Clean San Diego to create the largest, international cleanup day. I Love A Clean San Diego leads the efforts for this day in San Diego County for the 42nd year in a row. Data collected from this event feeds into the world’s largest database on marine debris. Join our efforts this year to be a part of this global event! This year, our countywide Coastal Cleanup Day will have around 100 + locations for volunteers to choose from, inland and coastal. Each site is hosted by a site captain (a specially trained volunteer) who will manage volunteers on site, properly dispose of the trash, and report back totals. Click the registration link below to see our main page for more details and the map of locations to choose from.

title: Slow Down Swap Party at the SD Climate Week People’s Summit
description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego for a FREE clothing swap at the SD Climate Week People's Summit. The County of San Diego's Rancho Los Penasquitos - In 1823, Francisco Maria Ruiz built a modest adobe that established Rancho Los Penasquitos as San Diego County's first Mexican land grant, remnants of which still stand within the present-day ranch house. Today, the surrounding park offers more than 10 miles of multi-use trails, a staging area, restrooms, and hosts weddings, reunions, and community events. The site also features the Los Peñasquitos Community Garden, founded in 2012, which provides 20 plots for local residents to grow their own produce through seasonal leases. The historic adobe serves as a cultural landmark and museum, offering visitors guided tours, educational programs, and a glimpse into the region’s early ranching and adobe life. title: Birding & Brews – Breakfast Edition
description: Be an Ocean Connector and start your day with birding and brews! Sip on smooth, nitro cold brew from Trident Coffee as you learn how their values of connection, community, and character align with our conservation mission. Then, head out on a guided walking tour along the Bayshore Bikeway at South San Diego Bay, equipped with binoculars and field guides. What to Expect: Coffee Tasting: Kick off the tour with a unique nitro cold brew tasting, crafted locally by Trident Coffee. Birdwatching Walk: Explore the Bayshore Bikeway with expert guides to spot native and migratory birds. Ecology & Conservation: Learn about local ecosystems and conservation efforts to protect our coastal wildlife. Best of all, every tour supports our education programs for South Bay youth!

title: North County Nature Paddle Eco Tour
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us on one of our North County Kayaking adventures! Discover the natural beauty of Agua Hedionda Lagoon on a guided kayaking tour through this unique coastal ecosystem in Carlsbad. As you paddle through calm waters near the lagoon’s wetlands, keep an eye out for snowy egrets, great egrets, and great blue herons foraging along the shoreline. You may also spot a belted kingfisher darting across the water, killdeer along the muddy upland edges, or red-tailed hawks perched high on the powerlines above. Along the way, learn about local conservation efforts, wetland restoration, and the rich biodiversity that makes this lagoon such an important habitat. This eco tour is suitable for all experience levels and offers a meaningful way to connect with nature while supporting environmental education and stewardship. What to Expect: Wildlife Viewing: Watch for native birds and other wildlife as you explore the lagoon by kayak. Educational Insights: Learn about lagoon ecology, restoration projects, and sustainable aquaculture. Birding Support: Binoculars and bird identification guides are provided to enhance your experience. Guided Exploration: Enjoy expert-led interpretation while paddling through this peaceful marine habitat. Join us for an inspiring on-the-water experience highlighting the importance of preserving San Diego’s coastal resources. Group Discounts available on 6 or more tickets. Email eco-tours@oceanconnectors.org

venue.name: California Watersports at Agua Hedionda Lagoon

title: Birding & Brews – Happy Hour Edition
description: Wind down your afternoon with birds, brews, and bayside views! Join us for a relaxed, guided birdwatching walk along the beautiful Bayshore Bikeway at South San Diego Bay. Then, wrap up the day with a complimentary craft beverage at Novo Brewing—perfect for happy hour with a conservation twist. What to Expect: Birdwatching Walk: Stroll the Bayshore Bikeway with expert guides to spot native and migratory birds in their natural habitat. Ecology & Conservation: Discover fascinating facts about the South Bay’s wildlife and ongoing efforts to protect our coastal ecosystems. Happy Hour Cheers: Finish your adventure with a refreshing drink at Novo Brewing, with non-alcoholic options available too—a great way to toast to community and conservation. Every Birding & Brews tour helps fund our education programs for South Bay youth—so you can enjoy your happy hour knowing you’re making a difference!

title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special. title: Science & Engineering Night 2026
description: Step into a world of hands-on exploration and discovery during a special evening packed with interactive activity booths, live science demos, and fun for the whole family! Saturday, July 18 | 5:00 p.m. - 7:30 p.m. Purchase Tickets   Through hands-on activity booths and an endless amount of fun, children will learn about: Automotive mechanics with Girlie Garage Coding with League of Amazing Programmers ​Robotics with Magikids Robotics ​​ Chemistry with Escondido Public Library Fire safety with Escondido Fire Department Evolution of technology with San Diego History Center Brain eye coordination with Fleet Science Center Microchips with ASML Competition robots with Team Rewind Engineering with PlayWell Teknologies Physics with USA Team Sport Stacking Math and banking with WestPac Wealth Partners Forensic science with AXEIS and Del Norte High School iGEM team ​Tinker a catapult ​Aerodynamics Science of our senses + more! Plus, you won’t want to miss mixing potions, coding spells, and working on optical illusions with LKW STEM Foundation ! Fill up with a quick bite from Epic Eatz food truck.   Pricing Price Type Members $17 Non-Members $20 Military $13 EBT/WIC/SNAP $7   Purchase Tickets   Event Reminders Please carefully read all event details before purchasing your tickets. Pre-registration is REQUIRED . Please bring digital or printed copies of all tickets on the day of the event. Tickets are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Event tickets are non-refundable and non-transferable . This event will take place rain or shine. Military and EBT/WIC tickets are required to bring proof of enrollment on the day of the event. If proof is not provided, visitors will be charged the full ticket price at check in. Food will be available for purchase from Epic Eatz food truck. All food and drink must be consumed outside in the Trudy Bronner Discovery Garden. Food is not allowed inside the Museum. All Admission tickets will grant admission to the Museum starting at 5:00 p.m. ALL individuals (adults and children) in your party MUST be registered and have a ticket to attend. Children must be accompanied by adults at all times   Sponsors: Science & Engineering Night is generously supported by: Activity Partners:

title: Music Pop-Up
description: Enjoy special music pop-up with Music n Me Music Together ! Kiddos will sing, dance, and make music with our special guests. Miss Keila is a working musician and experienced early childhood music educator who has been teaching Music Together® for over 10 years. She loves working with toddlers and families, bringing music into everyday life through singing, movement, and playful musical exploration. In Miss Keila’s sessions, families sing, move, and play together while building basic music skills that support creativity, speech development, coordination, and confidence. Children learn best through joyful interaction, and caregivers are encouraged to participate right alongside them—making music a shared experience. Expect lots of fun, laughter, and musical play with bells, scarves, shakers, and of course… the parachute! These interactive demos are designed to spark curiosity, encourage active learning, and leave families smiling long after the music ends.

title: Music Pop-Up
description: Enjoy special music pop-up with Music n Me Music Together ! Kiddos will sing, dance, and make music with our special guests. Miss Keila is a working musician and experienced early childhood music educator who has been teaching Music Together® for over 10 years. She loves working with toddlers and families, bringing music into everyday life through singing, movement, and playful musical exploration. In Miss Keila’s sessions, families sing, move, and play together while building basic music skills that support creativity, speech development, coordination, and confidence. Children learn best through joyful interaction, and caregivers are encouraged to participate right alongside them—making music a shared experience. Expect lots of fun, laughter, and musical play with bells, scarves, shakers, and of course… the parachute! These interactive demos are designed to spark curiosity, encourage active learning, and leave families smiling long after the music ends.

title: Music Pop-Up
description: Enjoy special music pop-up with Music n Me Music Together ! Kiddos will sing, dance, and make music with our special guests. Miss Keila is a working musician and experienced early childhood music educator who has been teaching Music Together® for over 10 years. She loves working with toddlers and families, bringing music into everyday life through singing, movement, and playful musical exploration. In Miss Keila’s sessions, families sing, move, and play together while building basic music skills that support creativity, speech development, coordination, and confidence. Children learn best through joyful interaction, and caregivers are encouraged to participate right alongside them—making music a shared experience. Expect lots of fun, laughter, and musical play with bells, scarves, shakers, and of course… the parachute! These interactive demos are designed to spark curiosity, encourage active learning, and leave families smiling long after the music ends.

title: Music Pop-Up
description: Enjoy special music pop-up with Music n Me Music Together ! Kiddos will sing, dance, and make music with our special guests. Miss Keila is a working musician and experienced early childhood music educator who has been teaching Music Together® for over 10 years. She loves working with toddlers and families, bringing music into everyday life through singing, movement, and playful musical exploration. In Miss Keila’s sessions, families sing, move, and play together while building basic music skills that support creativity, speech development, coordination, and confidence. Children learn best through joyful interaction, and caregivers are encouraged to participate right alongside them—making music a shared experience. Expect lots of fun, laughter, and musical play with bells, scarves, shakers, and of course… the parachute! These interactive demos are designed to spark curiosity, encourage active learning, and leave families smiling long after the music ends.

title: Fun Animal Friday
description: Discover new, furry friends with different animal partners every month! Pre-registration is not required. Free with Museum admission. Opportunities to pet the animals are limited and timed tickets will be provided upon check-in. **Petting is entirely contingent upon the animals’ well-being. We recommend arriving at the Museum early to maximize your chances of meeting the animals. A Hold Harmless form is required to be filled out for all guests who want to pet the animals.
